thm:1.2.summary: $v$-topology is subcanonical: for $X = \mathrm{Spa}(R,R^+)$ affinoid perfectoid, $H^0_v(X, \mathcal{O}_X) = R$, $H^0_v(X, \mathcal{O}_X^+) = R^+$, $H^i_v(X, \mathcal{O}_X) = 0$ for $i>0$, and $H^i_v(X, \mathcal{O}_X^+)$ almost zero. The descent theorem licensing the diamond formalism.

thm:1.5.summary: Diamond functor: a natural functor $X \mapsto X^\diamondsuit$ from analytic adic spaces over $\mathbb{Z}_p$ to locally spatial diamonds, satisfying $X_{\mathrm{\acute{e}t}} \cong X^\diamondsuit_{\mathrm{\acute{e}t}}$. The bridge from classical adic geometry into the diamond category.

thm:1.8.summary: Six-functor compatibilities for $f: Y \to X$ of small $v$-stacks: tensor compatibility $f^* A \otimes^{\mathbb{L}} f^* B = f^*(A \otimes^{\mathbb{L}} B)$; projection formulas via $Rf_* R\mathcal{H}om$. The category-theoretic apparatus the rest of the paper builds.

thm:1.9.summary: Base change in cartesian diagrams: smooth + qcqs base change for $D^+_{\mathrm{\acute{e}t}}$, proper base change for compactifiable. The structural commutation rules of the six-functor formalism.

thm:1.10.summary: Cohomological smoothness: locality, source-transfer, composition, base change for $\ell$-cohomologically smooth maps of small $v$-stacks. The structure theorem for smooth morphisms.

thm:1.11.summary: Constructibility under $Rf_!$: for quasicompact $\ell$-cohomologically smooth $f$ and $A \in D_{\mathrm{cons}}$, $Rf_! A \in D_{\mathrm{cons}}$. Finiteness theorem for the lower-shriek functor.

thm:1.12.summary: Poincaré duality for diamonds: for separated $\ell$-cohomologically smooth $X / \mathrm{Spa}(C, \mathcal{O}_C)$ over alg-closed nonarchimedean char-$p$ $C$ and $A \in D_{\mathrm{\acute{e}t}}(X, \mathbb{F}_\ell)$, $\mathcal{R}\mathcal{H}om$/Verdier duality holds. The arithmetic-geometric payoff.

thm:1.13.summary: Base-change invariance under extension of algebraically closed base fields: pullback $D_{\mathrm{\acute{e}t}}(X, \Lambda) \to D_{\mathrm{\acute{e}t}}(X', \Lambda)$ is fully faithful for discrete alg-closed $k'/k$ in char $p$. Solidity of the étale theory under base extension.

thm:1.19.summary: Several prestacks are $v$-stacks: affinoid perfectoid spaces over totally disconnected base; separated pro-étale perfectoid over strictly totally disconnected base; separated étale maps over all perfectoid spaces. The descent theorems making the diamond category work.

thm:5.8.summary: Zariski closed immersions $f: Z \to X = \mathrm{Spa}(R, R^+)$ are strongly Zariski closed. Lets Zariski-closed immersions be characterized by their structure-presheaf behavior.

thm:6.1.summary: Almost purity (imported): for $R$ perfectoid Tate with tilt $R^\flat$, finite étale $R$-algebras $S$ are perfectoid, and tilting induces equivalences of finite étale categories. Imported from Faltings, Kedlaya–Liu, Scholze (Perfectoid Spaces).

thm:6.3.summary: Étale-site tilting (imported): $X_{\mathrm{\acute{e}t}} \cong X^\flat_{\mathrm{\acute{e}t}}$ for any perfectoid space, with affinoid cohomology vanishing in higher degrees. The KL / Scholze input to the diamond framework.

thm:8.7.summary: Structure presheaves $\mathcal{O}, \mathcal{O}^+$ on the $v$-site are small sheaves and $v$-site is subcanonical. The descent theorem at the heart of thm:1.2.

thm:14.12.summary: Pro-étale to étale descent: for $A$ on $Y_{\mathrm{qpro\acute{e}t}}$ or $Y_v$, if $f^* A$ lifts to $D^+(Y'_{\mathrm{\acute{e}t}})$ along a $v$-cover, then $A$ lifts to $D^+(Y_{\mathrm{\acute{e}t}})$. The descent input for the étale-vs-pro-étale comparison.

thm:16.1.summary: Pullback compatibility for sheaves on quasi-pro-étale sites that come from étale ones: the sheaf-pullback diagram commutes with the étale-pro-étale comparison. Underwrites $f^*$ on the six-functor side.

thm:19.2.summary: $j_! Rg_*$ compatibility: for proper $f: Y' \to Y$, open $j: U \subset Y$, the natural transformation $j_! Rg_* \to Rf_* j'_!$ exists. Compatibility of proper-pushforward with open-extension — input to the six-functor formalism.

thm:22.5.summary: Compactification: for a compactifiable map representable in spatial diamonds with $\dim \mathrm{trg} f < \infty$ and prime-to-$p$ torsion coefficients, $Rf_!$ is well-defined as $Rf_* j_!$ along the canonical compactification. Body construction of $Rf_!$ — the foundation of thm:1.11.

thm:24.1.summary: Smoothness and dualizing complex of the ball: for $f: \mathbb{B} \to *$ from the ball to the point, $f$ is $\ell$-cohomologically smooth for $\ell \neq p$ with $D_f = Rf^! \Lambda \cong \Lambda(1)[2]$. The local-model computation underwriting cohomological smoothness.

thm:25.1.summary: Body Poincaré duality: for separated $\ell$-cohomologically smooth $X$ over $\mathrm{Spa}(C, \mathcal{O}_C)$ ($C$ alg-closed char $p$), full duality formalism with twist and shift. The body theorem proving thm:1.12.

edges.note: $v$-topology subcanonicity is exactly the structure-sheaf small-sheaf statement.
Diamond functor lands in $v$-stacks; thm:1.19 supplies the relevant descent.
Étale-site equivalence used to transport étale topos through $X \mapsto X^\diamondsuit$.
Six-functor compatibilities are stated jointly with base-change rules.
Local model: smoothness of the ball anchors the structural smoothness theory.
Constructibility of $Rf_!$ relies on the compactification construction.
Poincaré duality intro statement is the body theorem.
Duality bootstraps from the dualizing-complex computation on the ball.
Compactification gives the $Rf_!$ leg of the duality formalism.
Smoothness theory needed for duality on smooth $X$.
Base-change-invariance uses the v-stack structure of perfectoid-space prestacks.
Almost purity is the input that makes the étale-prestack v-stack.
$v$-sheaf property of structure presheaves uses the étale-site tilting cohomology vanishing.
Affinoid-perfectoid v-stack property feeds the structure-sheaf descent.
Zariski-closed immersions interact with $v$-sheafiness of structure presheaves.
Almost purity precedes étale-site tilting.
Quasi-pro-étale to étale lifting uses the structure-sheaf descent.
Pullback-compatibility runs after the descent statement is in place.
$j_! Rg_*$ compatibility is part of the six-functor formalism's commutation rules.
Compactification's $Rf_!$ definition uses the proper / open-immersion compatibility.

main_results.description: Section 1. Subcanonicity, diamond functor, six-functor / base-change / smoothness / constructibility / Poincaré duality / base-change invariance.

v_topology_and_descent.description: Sections 5–8. Zariski-closed immersions, almost-purity import, étale-site tilting, $v$-site subcanonicity. The descent infrastructure.

etale_pro_etale_comparison.description: Sections 14, 16. Quasi-pro-étale to étale lifting and pullback compatibilities.

six_functor_formalism.description: Sections 19, 22. Open-immersion / proper-pushforward compatibility and $Rf_!$ via compactification.

smoothness_and_duality.description: Sections 24, 25. Local model on the ball + body Poincaré duality.

Scholze:Perfectoid.title: Perfectoid spaces
Scholze:Perfectoid.role: Foundation: perfectoid spaces, almost purity, étale-site tilting (thm:6.1, thm:6.3 imported).

KedlayaLiu1.title: Relative p-adic Hodge theory: Foundations
KedlayaLiu1.role: Almost purity (thm:5.5.9) and finite-étale tensor equivalence imported at thm:6.1.

FaltingsAlmostEtaleExtensions.title: Almost étale extensions
FaltingsAlmostEtaleExtensions.role: Faltings's almost-purity theorem; first historical version imported at thm:6.1.

SGA4.title: Théorie des topos et cohomologie étale des schémas

Huber:Adic.title: Étale Cohomology of Rigid Analytic Varieties and Adic Spaces
Huber:Adic.role: Adic spaces, étale topology — the geometric category diamonds extend.
